title: calendula persica subsp. gracilis
intro: Calendula persica subsp. gracilis, also known as Persian marigold or pot marigold, is a subspecies of the Calendula persica plant, which belongs to the daisy family and is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The health benefits of Calendula persica subsp. gracilis include wound healing, skin protection, and soothing digestive issues, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. Calendula preparations often involve infusing the flowers in oil or water to create topical creams, ointments, and teas, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, from minor cuts and burns to more severe skin conditions and digestive problems.
